summary: Monday arXiv listings after the weekend emphasize relativistic-hydrodynamic shock regularity, heavy-quark nonequilibrium transport, light-ion flow constraints from ATLAS, gluon-saturation context, charge-resolved hyperon-polarization probes, and HEP/physics ML methods for generative collider modeling, robust tracking, corrected Boltzmann sampling, and differentiable control.
strongest_paper: 2607.15756: Israel-Stewart shock regularity breakdown is the sharpest new heavy-ion theory result; 2509.05171 remains the strongest experimental item in the current arXiv cycle despite being a revision.

papers.title: Breakdown of Smooth Shock Solutions in Transient Relativistic Hydrodynamics
papers.authors: Davi D. de Oliveira, Gabriel S. Denicol
papers.abstract: Israel-Stewart shock solutions lose regularity above a critical shock velocity, producing a subshock; third-order and numerical-viscosity regularizations extend the continuous-solution domain.

papers.title: Nonequilibrium approach to heavy-quark transport
papers.abstract: A Kadanoff-Baym nonequilibrium Green's-function formulation with HTL-resummed self energies reduces in the quasiparticle limit to Boltzmann transport with elastic scattering and medium-induced gluon emission.

papers.title: Measurement of the azimuthal anisotropy of charged particles in sqrt(sNN)=5.36 TeV O+O and Ne+Ne collisions with the ATLAS detector
papers.abstract: ATLAS measures v2-v4 in O+O and Ne+Ne with two-particle template subtraction and four-particle subevent cumulants, finding flow hierarchies and Ne+Ne deformation sensitivity.

papers.title: Charge-Odd Hyperon Polarization from Magnetic Spin Precession
papers.abstract: The paper predicts Lambda/anti-Lambda polarization splittings from opposite Larmor precession of strange and antistrange quarks in noncentral heavy-ion magnetic fields.

papers.title: Gluon Saturation
papers.abstract: A review of saturation, semiclassical limits, QCD evolution, Sudakov effects, coherent states, nuclear shadowing, heavy-ion collisions, and Drell-Yan-like processes.

papers.title: Learning Standard Model structure from LHC data with Riemannian flow matching
papers.authors: Midori Kato, Kevin A. Urquia-Calderon, Inar Timiryasov, Oleg Ruchayskiy
papers.abstract: ShellFlow trains a transformer conditional flow-matching model on about 10^9 ATLAS Open Data events and reports recovery of resonances, electroweak masses, and correlations from limited physics priors.

papers.title: Mitigating Detector Ageing Effects with Graph-Based Multi-Modal Track Reconstruction at Belle II
papers.authors: Lea Reuter, Tristan Brandes, Giacomo De Pietro, Torben Ferber
papers.abstract: A GNN/object-condensation track finder is tested under realistic Belle II detector ageing and reduces efficiency loss relative to baseline tracking while maintaining higher purity.

papers.title: Neural Non-Equilibrium Hamiltonian Monte Carlo for Corrected Boltzmann Sampling
papers.abstract: NHMC learns nonequilibrium Hamiltonian-style paths to Boltzmann targets and corrects estimates using path work, with tests on double-well and lattice phi^4 targets.

papers.title: Physics-enhanced reinforcement learning for real-time optimal control of dynamical systems
papers.authors: Matteo Tomasetto, Nicolo Botteghi, Gabriele Bruni, Andrea Manzoni
papers.abstract: PEARL combines RL with adjoint sensitivities and differentiable dynamics to improve sample efficiency in high-dimensional parametric control, demonstrated on unsteady-flow navigation problems.

papers.title: JetCoRD: Reliability-Aware Cross-Experiment Distillation of Jet Taggers with Adaptive Corrective Representation
papers.authors: Liu-Long Gao, Zheng-Kun Huang, Xiao-Wei Jiang, Jia-Feng Li, Gong-Xing Sun
papers.abstract: An 82k-parameter student is distilled from ATLAS GN2 and CMS ParT style teachers with reliability weighting, teacher repair, and gating, reporting gains at high-purity working points.
